$description: Brand image guidelines for the Yogananda ecosystem. Covers photography, illustrations, and generated imagery. Key constraint: PRI-01 prohibits AI generation of any media representing Yogananda or the lineage gurus. All SRF photographs are copyrighted.

copyright.notice: All photographs of Paramahansa Yogananda, the lineage gurus, SRF properties, and SRF events are copyrighted by Self-Realization Fellowship. Authorization required for any use.
copyright.contact: SRF must authorize use of any photographic assets.

lineage-gurus.$description: The spiritual lineage. Photographs exist for all four gurus. Authorization required from SRF.

figures.name: Mahavatar Babaji
figures.note: Fewer photographs available. The most sacred representations.

usage-rules: Never crop, filter, colorize, or digitally alter photographs of the gurus
Never use AI to generate, enhance, upscale, or modify guru images (PRI-01)
Never use guru images as decorative backgrounds or textures
Always display at respectful size — never thumbnails smaller than 120px
Always include proper attribution: 'Photo courtesy of Self-Realization Fellowship'
Never place guru images adjacent to commercial content or advertising
Images should appear in reverential context only — biography pages, about sections, not search results or navigation

placeholder-approach.$description: Until SRF authorizes specific photographs, use text-based representations or the lotus motif. Never use stock photos or AI-generated placeholders for guru images.
placeholder-approach.guru-pages: Full name in display typography, with biographical text. The lotus motif as visual anchor.
placeholder-approach.about-page: Lotus motif + text description of the lineage.

primary.description: Gold lotus on transparent background. 33x23 viewBox. Two-path SVG — upper petals and lower petals.
primary.usage: Browser favicon, PWA icon, social sharing fallback

apple-touch-icon.description: Gold lotus on navy background. 180x180 PNG generated from lotus SVG.

yss-variant.note: YSS may use a different emblem or lotus variant. Requires YSS input (PRO-043).

lotus-motif.$description: DES-016: Lotus as Unified Visual Motif. The lotus appears throughout the portal as a section divider, loading indicator, ornamental accent, and favicon. It is the single visual symbol connecting all parts of the experience.

applications.treatment: Gold at decorative opacity (0.4), centered, with generous vertical margin
Gold at ambient opacity (0.3), gentle pulse (not rotation)
Gold at decorative opacity (0.4), centered below chapter ending
Gold at ambient opacity (0.3), centered above 'no results' message

never: Never rotate the lotus (it should always be upright — it's a spiritual symbol)
Never use as a background pattern or repeating tile
Never apply filters or effects (drop shadow, glow, etc.)

botanical-glyphs.$description: Extended lotus motif system. 12 distinct botanical illustrations — 11 from yogananda.org, 1 promoted registration mark — optimized to currentColor SVGs. Themed via CSS mask-image — color set by the design system, not the SVG. Five color voices: sacred (gold), growth (green), devotion (blue), warmth (orange), crimson (red).

shape-groups.banner: lotus-05, lotus-06, lotus-08 — wide, ideal for dividers
shape-groups.landscape: lotus-02, lotus-04, lotus-07 — moderate width, dividers or ornaments
shape-groups.square: lotus-01, lotus-03, lotus-09, lotus-11, lotus-12 — balanced, versatile
shape-groups.portrait: lotus-10 — taller than wide, unique focal point

never: Same constraints as canonical lotus — never rotate, never tile, never scale below 16px, never apply effects
Never mix color voices within a single section — pick one voice per editorial context
Never use all 12 glyphs on a single page — select 2–3 for visual consistency

illustrations.$description: The portal currently uses no custom illustrations. If illustrations are added in the future, they should follow these guidelines.
illustrations.style: Line art in SRF Gold or Navy. Simple, elegant, non-photorealistic. The illustration style should echo woodcut or engraving traditions — scholarly, not whimsical.
illustrations.subjects: Natural motifs (lotus, mountains, water), geometric patterns (mandalas, yantras for YSS), architectural elements (temple domes, library arches)
illustrations.never: Never illustrate the gurus. Never create cartoon or stylized versions. Never use AI-generated illustrations of spiritual figures.

social-sharing.$description: Open Graph and social media card images. Used when portal links are shared on social platforms.

default.approach: Gold lotus centered on navy background with portal title. Simple, dignified.

per-chapter.approach: Chapter title in display typography on navy background with gold lotus ornament. No book cover images (copyright).
per-chapter.note: Per-page social images are a future feature. Default image is sufficient initially.
